growth_records.medium: RPMI 1640 + 10% FCS + 10 mM glucose (intracellular in Caco-2 cells)

up.units: µmol assimilated per 2e8 intracellular bacteria per 6.5 h

growth_records.cite: Götz A et al. Carbon Metabolism of Enterobacterial Human Pathogens Growing in Epithelial Colorectal Adenocarcinoma (Caco-2) Cells. PLoS ONE (2010). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0010586. [PMC2868055]
growth_records.snippet: After the 6.5 h infection period, the intracellular 2×10^8 EIEC-1 and 10^7 EIEC-2 bacteria incorporated 14C-label corresponding to 0.8 µmol and 0.3 µmol glucose, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Growth rate CALCULATED from intracellular cfu (Table 1, gentamicin-protection): mu=ln(cfu_harvest/cfu_1h)/Δt=ln(2.3e8/1.9e6)/(6.5-1)h=ln(121.05)/5.5=0.872 /h; td=ln2/0.872=0.795 h (47.7 min). Uptake is 14C-glucose ASSIMILATION into biomass (some C respired to CO2), reported per 2e8 bacteria per 6.5 h; NOT convertible to mmol/gDW/h because paper gives no bacterial dry weight. 13C-isotopologue profiling ([U-13C6]glucose, 10 mM) showed EIEC HN280 in cytosol uses glucose (not glucose-6P, gluconate or fatty acids) as major C source, with flux via glycolysis/Entner-Doudoroff, PPP, TCA and PEP<->OAA anaplerosis (qualitative, no quantitative net fluxes reported → mfa_fluxes empty). Full RPMI 1640 composition in supplementary table (not embedded).

growth_records.medium: RPMI 1640 + 10% FCS + 10 mM glucose (intracellular in Caco-2 cells)

up.units: µmol assimilated per 1e7 intracellular bacteria per 6.5 h

growth_records.cite: Götz A et al. Carbon Metabolism of Enterobacterial Human Pathogens Growing in Epithelial Colorectal Adenocarcinoma (Caco-2) Cells. PLoS ONE (2010). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0010586. [PMC2868055]
growth_records.snippet: there were 2×10^8 intracellular EIEC-1 bacteria but only 3×10^7 EIEC-2 bacteria ... incorporated 14C-label corresponding to 0.8 µmol and 0.3 µmol glucose, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Growth rate CALCULATED from intracellular cfu (Table 1): mu=ln(3.4e7/5.6e5)/5.5=ln(60.71)/5.5=0.747 /h; td=0.928 h (55.7 min). Uptake = 14C-glucose assimilation, 0.3 µmol per 1e7 bacteria per 6.5 h; per-bacterium consumption roughly equal to EIEC-1 (paper). Not convertible to mmol/gDW/h (no dry weight given). 13C data: EIEC 4608-58 uses glucose PLUS a C3-compound (e.g. glycerol/pyruvate/lactate) as additional carbon source; pathway usage qualitative only.

growth_records.medium: RPMI 1640 + 10% FCS + 10 mM glucose (intracellular in Caco-2 cells)
growth_records.cite: Götz A et al. Carbon Metabolism of Enterobacterial Human Pathogens Growing in Epithelial Colorectal Adenocarcinoma (Caco-2) Cells. PLoS ONE (2010). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0010586. [PMC2868055]
growth_records.snippet: The replication rates of the glucose-6P uptake mutants of all three pathogens were comparable to those of the corresponding wild-type strains.
growth_records.notes: Growth rate CALCULATED from intracellular cfu (Table 1, ΔuhpT column): mu=ln(1.7e8/9.7e5)/5.5=ln(175.3)/5.5=0.939 /h; td=0.738 h (44.3 min). Comparable to EIEC-1 WT (0.872 /h) — confirms glucose-6-phosphate is NOT a relevant intracellular carbon source. No strain-specific 14C glucose-uptake value reported for this mutant.
